page: Microsoft Azure D32_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dv4-series, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Cache resources consist of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 35 MB L3 cache. The CPU supports AVX, AVX-512 Foundation, and AVX-512 Vector Neural Network Instructions (VNNI), alongside AES cryptography and virtualization extensions.

Benchmark results place the D32_v4 consistently in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. In CPU-intensive tasks, it achieves a stress-ng multi-core score of 29,790.94 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 10,337.0. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 1,662.13 GB/sec, which drops to 86.61 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Database operations, measured via PassMark, score at 11,185.66, while Redis SET operations reach 26.15 million operations per second.

A key architectural tradeoff is the complete absence of local NVMe or SSD storage, meaning all storage must be network-attached. Additionally, there are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses and no GPU accelerators. While the system handles small to medium language model inference adequately, achieving 45.3 tokens/sec prompt processing on a 7B model, it exhibits poor performance on larger models, with prompt processing for a 70B model dropping to 4.36 tokens/sec. This profile makes the instance suitable for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and database operations that do not require local high-speed storage or GPU acceleration.
description: The Microsoft Azure D32_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The x86_64 architecture supports AVX-512 instructions but lacks local storage and GPU acceleration. Across standard benchmarks, the instance delivers mid-tier performance, with a multi-core Geekbench score of 10,337.0 and a Redis throughput of 26.15 million operations per second. However, large-scale LLM inference is a notable bottleneck, with 70B model prompt processing falling into the bottom 10% tier. This virtual machine is designed for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, and static web serving where network-attached storage is sufficient.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering balanced mid-tier performance for standard enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, offering balanced compute and memory without local storage.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and 128 GB of RAM for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with AVX-512 and VNNI instruction support
No local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring network-attached storage
Mid-tier benchmark performance for database operations and software compilation
Suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, and Redis deployments
